fields.description:
# Create canonical AGENTS.md (rebar dev guide) + reduce CLAUDE.md to a thin @AGENTS.md pointer

## Why

`AGENTS.md` is read natively by Codex, Cursor, Copilot's coding agent, Zed, Windsurf, Amp,
Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, and Cline — so one canonical `AGENTS.md` covers the ecosystem,
and Claude Code is bridged by a one-line import. This also fixes the size/duplication
anti-pattern: sections that duplicate a `docs/` file become one-line pointers.

## What

Author a lean `AGENTS.md` at the repo root that keeps rebar-INTERNAL developer specifics inline
and replaces every already-documented topic with a one-line `docs/…md` pointer. Then reduce
`CLAUDE.md` to a single `@AGENTS.md` import plus an optional `## Claude Code` section.

- **INLINE (kept in full):** intro; the record-work/claim-before-you-work operating rules; the
  parallel-agent loop; env bootstrap (`make install`/`make hooks`); module-size policy; Serena
  navigation; a **condensed** Git/Gerrit section; a 6-line library snippet.
- **POINTER (one line each), to EXISTING docs:** gate protocols → `docs/plan-review-gate.md`;
  quality gates + ticket template + project criteria → `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md`; MCP
  tool set → `docs/mcp-reference.md`; store-shares-writes + parent-first cascade →
  `docs/concurrency.md`; ticket model → `docs/ticket-model.md` (created by S1); session logs →
  `docs/event-schema.md` + `docs/user-guide.md`; library reference → `docs/reuse-surface.md`; the
  LLM agent-operations topic → `docs/llm-framework.md`; the completion close-gate →
  `docs/plan-review-gate.md`. These are POINTERS only — this story writes no content into those
  docs (their content already exists; the two "stowaway" topics currently mixed into CLAUDE.md's
  Session-logs heading are simply not carried into AGENTS.md).

## Scope

In: new `AGENTS.md`, rewritten `CLAUDE.md`. No writes to any `docs/*` file (pointers only). Out:
`CLAUDE.local.md` (S3), the shim/reference sweep (S6), and code. **Depends on S1** (already linked
`depends_on`): `docs/ticket-model.md` must exist before `AGENTS.md` points at it. Rollback is a
plain `git revert` (docs/text only).

## Design decisions (spike-validated)

- The `@AGENTS.md` import is PROVEN in Claude Code 2.1.211 (headless canary + official docs
  recommend exactly `@AGENTS.md` + `## Claude Code`); no symlink fallback.
- Imports "load at launch" and do not reduce context, so the token win comes from de-dup into docs
  and **AGENTS.md must itself be lean.** A grounded estimate puts a complete lean file at ~152
  lines and ≤ 200 only if the Git/Gerrit section is condensed to agent-actionable rules and defers
  the full recipe to `CONTRIBUTING.md` (verbatim Gerrit → ~215-230, which misses the target).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AGENTS.md` exists at repo root and is ≤ 200 lines.
  - Proving: `test -f AGENTS.md && [ "$(wc -l < AGENTS.md)" -le 200 ]`
- [ ] The Git/Gerrit section is condensed (≤ ~55 lines) and defers the full recipe to
  `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
  - Proving: `rg -q 'CONTRIBUTING.md' AGENTS.md`; reviewer confirms the section ≤ 55 lines.
- [ ] Each already-documented topic is a one-line pointer, not a restated section.
  - Proving: `rg -n 'docs/plan-review-gate.md|docs/mcp-reference.md|docs/ticket-model.md|docs/concurrency.md|docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md|docs/llm-framework.md' AGENTS.md`
- [ ] Every `docs/…md` path that `AGENTS.md` points at resolves to a real file (no dangling
  pointer; targets created by S1 are in place by then).
  - Proving: `for p in $(rg -o 'docs/[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.md' AGENTS.md | sort -u); do test -f "$p" || { echo "MISSING $p"; exit 1; }; done`
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` is a single `@AGENTS.md` import + optional `## Claude Code` section, ≤ 30 lines.
  - Proving: `[ "$(rg -c '@AGENTS.md' CLAUDE.md)" = 1 ] && [ "$(wc -l < CLAUDE.md)" -le 30 ]`
- [ ] `make lint` passes.
  - Proving: `make lint`
